truths_recorded.locked_summary: Ancestors fled an ancient, nameless darkness that devoured Old World kingdoms. The darkness was escaped, not destroyed.
Cold iron repels the ancient darkness and its servants. Iron is used in all protective charms, doorways, and palisades. Vows sworn on iron are inviolable.
The Ironlands had a prior civilisation — the First People — whose ruins dot the highlands and forests. Iron wards fail within those ruins.
The Ironlands resist settlement with brutal winters, oversized predators, and treacherous rivers. The land appears to grow wilder as settlement expands.
Two categories of horror: servants of the Old World darkness, and native terrors predating human settlement. They compete with each other at the edges of settled land.
No gods crossed the ocean. The Ironlands have spirits in stones, rivers, trees, and storms. Bone-Singers commune with them. First People ruins are spirit-nodes.
All magic requires a price paid to spirits: memories, years of life, secrets, or impossible vows. Bone-Singers are visibly diminished by their practice.
Giants are solitary, ancient, powerful, and irritated by settlers. Varou are wolf-blooded pack-runners with complex territorial codes settlers often violate.
No cities or towns. Only small steadings — a few families behind palisades. Nearest neighbours may be a full day away. Self-reliance is survival.
Authority earned through completing iron vows. Oath-keepers command respect anywhere. Oath-breakers are socially diminished — a severe punishment in a sparse world.
No professional soldiers. Every adult is expected to carry iron and fight. The Ironsworn are the sharpest of ordinary people who have sworn great vows.

entities_created.name: The Old World
entities_created.description: The civilisation left behind. Destroyed by the Ancient Darkness. Details suppressed or forgotten.

entities_created.name: The Ancient Darkness
entities_created.description: Ancient nameless force that destroyed the Old World. Servants crossed the ocean alongside settlers. Repelled by cold iron.

entities_created.name: The First People
entities_created.description: Unknown pre-settler civilisation. Left ruins in highlands and forests. Iron wards do not function within their ruins.

entities_created.name: The Giants
entities_created.description: Ancient solitary beings of enormous power in the deep wilderness. Regard settlers as an irritating novelty.

entities_created.name: The Varou
entities_created.description: Wolf-blooded pack-runners controlling deep forests under complex territorial codes.

entities_created.name: Bone-Singers
entities_created.description: Magic practitioners who bargain with land spirits. Every working exacts a visible cost — premature aging, inexplicable scars.

entities_created.name: Iron Vows
entities_created.description: Central social contract. Vows sworn on cold iron are inviolable. Fulfilling them grants authority; breaking them is social death.

entities_created.name: The Ruins of the First People
entities_created.description: Scattered pre-settler ruins. Spirit-world and living-world overlap here. Iron wards fail within them. Sacred and dangerous.
